## 🧠 CORE LESSONS (SELF-CORRECTION)

# 🧠 Agent Lessons

> This file is a self-optimizing loop. When an AI agent makes a mistake and you correct it, tell the agent to document the pattern here so it mathematically never repeats the mistake.

## 📋 Lessons
- Ensure all variables are typed before pushing.

## Known issues

- Never force push to main, master, or production branches
- Don't commit .env, secrets, API keys, or credentials — add to .gitignore
- Don't leave console.log, print(), debugger, or TODO in production code
- Don't hardcode API keys, passwords, database URLs, or secrets anywhere
- Never use eval(), exec(), or Function() with user input
- Sanitize ALL user input before database queries — prevent SQL injection
- Don't use innerHTML — use textContent or sanitized rendering
- Don't store passwords in plain text — use bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt
- Use parameterized queries — never string concatenation for SQL
- Never store session tokens in localStorage — use httpOnly cookies

## Project patterns

- convention in .gitignore
- convention in .gitignore
- what-changed in requirements.txt — confirmed 3x
- convention in .gitignore
- Don't commit node_modules, __pycache__, build/, dist/ — add to .gitignore
- Use meaningful commit messages in imperative mood: "Add feature" not "Added feature"
- Always pull before push to avoid conflicts
- Remove unused imports, variables, and dead code
- Keep functions under 50 lines and single-responsibility
- Handle edge cases: empty arrays, empty strings, null, undefined, 0, NaN
